statute: DC Code § 47-2881 et seq.; DC Municipal Regulations Title 16
license_label: DC Basic Business License Number (General Contractor or Home Improvement)
license_notes: ALL contractors in DC must hold a Basic Business License. Home Improvement Contractors need HI-specific BBL. Unlicensed contractors and those who hire unlicensed subs both face $2,000 minimum fines. No lien rights for unlicensed contractors.

mandatory_clauses.label: DC BBL license disclosure
DC tax registration
mandatory_clauses.text: DC Office of Tax and Revenue Registration Number: {{tax_reg_number}}

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Class 1 infraction — minimum $2,000 per offense. Both GC and unlicensed sub penalized. No lien rights for unlicensed contractors.
notes: DC requires BBL for ALL contractors. Home Improvement Salesperson BBL for residential work. Businesses must register with Office of Tax and Revenue. Electrical, plumbing, HVAC require additional trade licenses. $10,000 bond required. Both the GC and any unlicensed subcontractor they hire receive fines.
